welcome.title: Welcome to Cogit
description: The goal of this project is to function as a comprehensive resource for chess improvement. Its contents
are written and edited to be concise, without omitting important details, and approachable
even to beginner players.
It is assumed that the reader knows the rules of the game, but nothing beyond that is required.
Individual moves are referenced using
, which is standard practice in the chess world.
For each move that is played on the interactive chess board, relevant information explaining its strengths
and drawbacks will be retrieved and shown in the
segment located directly above this article.
Commentary is bespoke for each possible move, in each board position. Due to the exponential
nature of chess, this means that it is not possible to cover every move that
be played in a given chess game; as such, the available commentary is focused primarily in common chess
openings, notable chess games, and custom positions that can be reached fairly quickly from those points.
The segment located directly underneath the chess board displays possible moves from the current board
position for which there is corresponding information (or for which a subsequent move has corresponding
information).
The segment in which this article is displayed is the
. In order to keep commentary lean, general information is arranged into glossary topics which can be
accessed via the margin to the left. When a particular topic is relevant or necessary to understand
the currently displayed commentary, a button which opens that topic will be embedded in the commentary.
